best_strategy.name: Strategy A: Minimum-M, Minimum-K Padding with Maximum Tile

metrics.npu_utilization: high (single large kernel fills NPU compute array)

best_strategy.rationale: With M=1537, the smallest valid Mp is 2048 (the only ALLOWED_M value >= 1537). With N=257, the smallest valid Np is 512 (since 256 < 257 and 512 is the next ALLOWED_N value >= 257). With K=769, the smallest valid Kp is 1024 (since 768 < 769 and 1024 is the next ALLOWED_K value >= 769). Using the maximum possible tile (2048, 512, 1024) achieves: (1) exactly 1 tile dispatch minimizing kernel launch overhead, (2) maximum arithmetic intensity of 292.6 ops/byte keeping NPU compute units saturated, (3) minimum padding by choosing the smallest valid padded shapes in each dimension.

alternative_strategies_considered.name: Strategy B: Larger Np=768
alternative_strategies_considered.verdict: REJECTED: Higher padding waste (431.6% vs 254.4%) outweighs marginal arithmetic intensity gain. N=257 pads to 768 wastes nearly 3x more N-dimension compute.

alternative_strategies_considered.name: Strategy C: Smaller tiles within A (tile_m=1024)
alternative_strategies_considered.verdict: REJECTED: Same padded shape as Strategy A but 2x tile dispatches and lower arithmetic intensity (204.8 vs 292.6). No benefit.

alternative_strategies_considered.name: Strategy D: Smaller K tile within A (tile_k=512)
alternative_strategies_considered.verdict: REJECTED: Same padded shape but 2x K-direction tiles, lower arithmetic intensity.

alternative_strategies_considered.name: Strategy E: Larger Kp=1536
alternative_strategies_considered.verdict: REJECTED: More padding waste than Strategy A (381.3% vs 254.4%) and 2 tiles required.

winner_key_insight: Mp=2048 is forced (only option >= 1537). The real tradeoffs are in N and K. For N=257, Np=512 is strictly better than Np=768 because it wastes less than half the N-padding compute. For K=769, Kp=1024 is the minimum valid choice. Using the maximum tile (2048,512,1024) achieves a single-dispatch, high-intensity kernel execution.
